title: Ptisana microcarpa

intro: Ptisana microcarpa, also known as the New Zealand horsetail or raurau, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Marattiales. This plant has been traditionally used in Māori medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ptisana microcarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Māori medicine, Ptisana microcarpa is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the form of a topical poultice to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
